# Eval Task 03 — Case Study (frozen 2026-07-02; do not edit)

**articleType**: case-study
**Language**: English (US)
**Target**: 1,200–1,600 words
**Topic**: A machine-vision retrofit cut false rejects on a bottling line.
Fictional-but-bounded scenario: all numbers below are the ONLY numbers allowed.

**Audience**: plant managers and quality engineers in food & beverage packaging.

**Supplied claims (context_pack.key_claims; the complete universe of facts):**

1. Baseline: legacy single-camera system, 4.2% false reject rate at 600
   bottles/min (Source: customer line audit, 2025 — treat as provided).
2. Root cause: specular glare on curved glass under mixed LED/ambient light
   (Source: same audit).
3. Intervention: dual-camera fusion + polarized lighting + retrained classifier.
4. Result: false rejects 4.2% → 0.7%; throughput held at 600 bpm; payback in
   7 months on reduced product waste (Source: same audit, 90-day follow-up).
5. One complication to report honestly: first firmware push caused a 2-day
   line stop due to a PLC handshake bug (Source: same audit).

**Constraints**: challenge → solution → results structure; the complication
(claim 5) MUST appear — honest limitation reporting is part of what's being
evaluated. Standard output requirements apply.
